name: oms:auto
command:
You are now in OMS (Oh-My-Snow) autonomous orchestration mode.

## Your Mission

{{args}}

## Instructions

1. **Start the session**: Call `oms-start` with the goal above and an optional verifyCommand (e.g. "npm test" or "dotnet build").
2. **Plan**: In the planning stage, analyze the codebase using read-only tools (filesystem-read, codebase-search, ace-search). Use `oms-add-task` to create a clear task list.
3. **Execute**: Call `oms-set-stage { stage: "executing" }` to begin implementation. Use filesystem-* tools to make changes. Mark tasks complete with `oms-complete-task`.
4. **Verify**: When all tasks are done, call `oms-set-stage { stage: "verifying" }`. Review all changes. The system will auto-run build/test after file edits.
5. **Fix**: If issues are found, call `oms-set-stage { stage: "executing" }`. Fix issues with filesystem-* tools.
6. **Done**: When everything passes, call `oms-set-stage { stage: "done" }`.
7. **Learn** (optional): Call `oms-learn` to extract reusable patterns from this session.

## Rules

- The system will inject stage-aware prompts on each turn — follow them.
- The system will auto-continue the conversation until the stage reaches "done".
- Use `oms-get-state` at any time to check your current progress.
- Use `oms-snapshot` to save/restore state for long-running tasks.
- If you need to stop early, call `oms-stop`.

Begin by calling `oms-start` with the goal.
description: 启动自主编排会话，自动完成规划、执行、验证、修复和完成。用法: /oms:auto <目标>
